summary: E2E validation of 1v1 Quick Match feature (backend + frontend). 8/8 backend pytest passed. Frontend UI flow end-to-end verified: Dashboard quick-1v1 link → /match/1v1/new → fill opponent/course/tee/holes → Send Challenge → /play/{courseId}?match={matchId}; buddy visits /match/1v1/{id} and can Accept (→ active + redirect to /play) or Decline.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~3300+ lines. Consider splitting /api/matches/1v1, /api/rounds, /api/scorecards, and /api/tournaments into separate routers for maintainability.

Match1v1Detail.js: when swapping auth tokens via SPA navigation, AuthContext may cache stale user; not a bug for real users but affects automated tests. Consider re-validating user on match detail load.
NewMatch1v1.js: no empty-state or 'no opponents found' message when opponent search returns []; minor UX gap.
Match1v1Detail.js: polling every 30s is fine but consider pausing when page is hidden to reduce load.
test_credentials: admin@fairway.com / FairwayAdmin123! (admin, from /app/memory/test_credentials.md); buddy@test.com / BuddyTest123! (player). Both verified via /api/auth/login.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed needed. Tests use existing course_992f69846733 (User-Added Test Course, Default tee, 9 holes). Pre/post cleanup deletes tournaments where is_1v1=true and scorecards where tournament_id starts with t1v1_.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use pymongo via mongodb://localhost:27017 db=test_database to clean stale 1v1 docs between runs. Only one pending/active 1v1 match allowed per (challenger, opponent) pair — expect 409 on duplicates. When testing detail page with SPA navigation, force page.goto() (full reload) after swapping fairway_token in localStorage so AuthContext re-reads the new token.

backend: POST /api/matches/1v1 creates match with is_1v1=true, status=pending
GET /api/matches/1v1/active returns match for both players
GET /api/matches/1v1/{id} returns p1_card + p2_card + course metadata
POST /api/rounds with tournament_id=t1v1_* mirrors to scorecards with total_strokes/total_to_par/status=submitted
Match auto-completes with correct result.winner_id when both players submit
frontend: /match/1v1/new renders with data-testid=new-1v1-page and all form controls
Send Challenge button → POST succeeds → redirect to /play/{courseId}?match={matchId}
Buddy sees Accept/Decline buttons when status=pending and they are player2
Accept → toast + redirect to /play + backend status=active
